name: huggingface-downloader
description: Fast, resumable HuggingFace model/dataset downloads via the hfdownloader CLI — grab models for ComfyUI straight from a huggingface.co link.

methods.name: github-release-unix
methods.command: mkdir -p ~/.local/bin && OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') && ARCH=$(uname -m | sed 's/x86_64/amd64/;s/aarch64/arm64/') && URL=$(curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/bodaay/HuggingFaceModelDownloader/releases/latest | grep browser_download_url | grep "${OS}_${ARCH}" | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4) && curl -sSL -o ~/.local/bin/hfdownloader "$URL" && chmod +x ~/.local/bin/hfdownloader

methods.name: github-release-windows
methods.command: mkdir -p "$HOME/bin" && URL=$(curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/bodaay/HuggingFaceModelDownloader/releases/latest | grep browser_download_url | grep windows_amd64 | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4) && curl -sSL -o "$HOME/bin/hfdownloader.exe" "$URL"

install.notes: Downloads the latest prebuilt binary from GitHub releases. The binary must be reachable on PATH as 'hfdownloader' ('hfdownloader.exe' on Windows). See the skill file for detailed install + PATH steps.

post_install_hints: Ensure the hfdownloader binary is on PATH. Linux/macOS: ~/.local/bin (add to PATH if it is not already). Windows: place hfdownloader.exe in a folder that is on the user PATH (for example a 'bin' folder inside your user profile).

IMPORTANT: gated or private HuggingFace models (Llama, Gemma, etc.) need an access token. Create one with read access at https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens, then set the HF_TOKEN environment variable to it. Public models download without a token.
Set COMFYUI_MODELS_DIR in ~/.claude/skills-config.sh to your ComfyUI models folder so the skill places downloads in the right location.

COMFYUI_MODELS_DIR.prompt: Path to your ComfyUI models directory (where downloaded models should be placed)

integration_test.description: Dry-run lists the repo file plan without downloading — confirms network and HuggingFace API access work
